title: Headache follow-up in GP
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Provides systemic, neurologic, onset, associated-feature, and pattern-change red-flag domains.
red_flags.rationale: The exact DHA adult red-flag section supports clinician-assessed systemic, neurological, onset, associated-feature, and pattern-change concern plus available examination without asserting absence.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Provides systemic, neurologic, onset, associated-feature, and pattern-change red-flag domains.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Headaches in Adults

red_flags.text: Supports recording headache frequency, duration, severity, intervention effectiveness, impact, and clinician-led further investigation or referral when red flags develop.
red_flags.rationale: The exact diary and reassessment section supports recording existing diary, frequency, duration, severity, intervention effectiveness, impact, results, clinician discussion, referral status, and follow-up without generating an investigation.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports recording headache frequency, duration, severity, intervention effectiveness, impact, and clinician-led further investigation or referral when red flags develop.

source.title: Headaches in over 12s: diagnosis and management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.6 — diary, reassessment, referral, and information

medication.text: Supports headache location, quality, severity, duration, frequency, activity impact, nausea, vomiting, light or sound sensitivity, aura, menstrual relation, and medication-overuse review.
medication.rationale: The exact headache-feature section supports interval location, quality, severity, duration, frequency, activity impact, nausea, vomiting, light or sound sensitivity, aura, menstrual relation, and medicine-overuse context without assigning headache type.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports headache location, quality, severity, duration, frequency, activity impact, nausea, vomiting, light or sound sensitivity, aura, menstrual relation, and medication-overuse review.

source_limitations: Current headache pattern, observations, neurological or eye examination, pregnancy, cause, diagnosis, diary content, medicine, test, referral, urgency, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
